Linnaea indica is a flowering plant, specifically a twining, semi-evergreen shrub native to the Himalayas. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine and has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Linnaea indica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions.
